mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate lowers the equity discount rate and supports valuation expansion, so months with declining FEDFUNDS predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than unconditional returns in months when FEDFUNDS is falling, or if the sign of the effect reverses in more than 60% of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment above its recent trend signals weakening household income and demand, which should depress near-term corporate earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes of rising UNRATE are followed by positive SPX 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the average 20-day return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales accelerate relative to the recent level, investors infer stronger consumer demand and better revenue growth, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases on a 3-month rolling basis
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth is not associated with positive 5-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate falls below 52% over any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: An expansion in industrial production together with stronger housing starts indicates broad real activity and cyclical earnings momentum, so the joint signal should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined INDPRO-HOUST signal does not outperform either series alone in predicting 20-day SPX returns, or if joint-positive episodes do not produce returns above baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A conjunction of low inflation and low policy rates supports multiple expansion by preserving real discount rates and limiting future tightening risk, so CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS together should forecast positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation remains below its 12-month average while FEDFUNDS stays below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if low-inflation, low-rate episodes do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction term is not positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A downturn in housing starts is an early warning of weaker construction activity, consumer spending, and credit-sensitive demand, so falling HOUST should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if sharp HOUST declines are followed by positive SPX 20-day returns in a majority of triggered cases, or if the signal has no explanatory power relative to random timing.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains combined with falling unemployment indicate broad labor-market strength and rising income expectations, which should support equity prices over the next 5 trading days through improved consumption prospects.
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-UNRATE joint signal fails to outperform either input alone, or if positive labor combinations are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, rising industrial production should dominate other signals because earnings revisions follow output recovery with a lag, so INDPRO upswings predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:cyclical recoveries typically lead earnings revisions
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO uptrends do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns in recovery regimes, or if the effect disappears outside of recoveries without offsetting strength inside them.
